title: Deep-Sea Cable Splice: Length-OOD Generalization
strategies: majority-class constant: predict 'not well-spliced' for every query (the strict majority) -> reproduces the evaluator's anchor baseline (~0.1); no generalization

count-balance heuristic: label well-spliced iff #opening tokens == #closing tokens; catches deleted-token cables but is blind to type mismatches and to the depth budget -> only marginally above the constant

recover the matcher: run a per-type stack matcher (a Dyck check over K bracket types) to generalize the matching rule to arbitrary length, and infer the depth budget conservatively from the deepest well-spliced training cable; nails the length-OOD split but under-estimates the unseen depth budget -> strong yet sub-perfect

aggressive depth extrapolation: same matcher but guess a larger depth budget (e.g. observed max + margin, or no cap at all) -- a different inductive bias that trades deep-valid recall against too-deep false-accepts; can exceed the conservative learner if the guess is right
